body:
### Problem

Hi there!

An error message shows when trying to look at document symbols using python-lsp-server:
```
vim.schedule callback: /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua:1955: attempt to concatenate field 'containerName' (a userdata value)
stack traceback:
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua:1955: in function 'map_result'
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/handlers.lua:294: in function 'handler'
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/buf.lua:180: in function 'handler'
        /usr/share/nvim/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/client.lua:742: in function ''
        vim/_core/editor.lua: in function <vim/_core/editor.lua:0>
```

This does not happen with any other LSP I've used recently.

The error is thrown in `vim.lsp.util.symbols_to_items()` when `symbol.containerName` is `vim.NIL`:
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/43398547ec72c99ab0b90837c02de4a0add77b04/runtime/lua/vim/lsp/util.lua#L1972

Probably related to this change in 0.12:
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/43398547ec72c99ab0b90837c02de4a0add77b04/runtime/doc/news.txt#L54-L55

I guess the fix is to check explicitly for `vim.NIL` instead of using the ternary operator. I don't have the time right now to familiarise myself with how to contribute that change myself but I hope this helps!

### Steps to reproduce using "nvim --clean -u minimal_init.lua"

```lua
-- minimal_init.lua
local pattern = "python"
local cmd = { "pylsp" }
local root_markers = { ".git", ".editorconfig" }
local settings = vim.empty_dict()

vim.api.nvim_create_autocmd("FileType", {
	pattern = pattern,
	callback = function(args)
		local match = vim.fs.find(root_markers, { path = args.file, upward = true })[1]
		local root_dir = match and vim.fn.fnamemodify(match, ":p:h") or nil
		vim.lsp.start({
			name = "pylsp",
			cmd = cmd,
			root_dir = root_dir,
			settings = settings,
		})
	end,
})

vim.lsp.set_log_level("debug")
```

1. Open Neovim `nvim --clean -u minimal_init.lua`.
2. Open an empty python file `:e test.py`.
3. Query document symbols `gO`.
4. Observe "No document symbols found".
5. Add code to the python file `import os`.
6. Query document symbols `gO`.
7. Observe the error message as above.

### Expected behavior

It should display the symbols as expected.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

v0.12.1

### Language server name/version

python-lsp-server 1.14.0-2

### Operating system/version

arch linux

### Log file

https://gist.github.com/mariusz-tang/78d362b2d7a2372469db6de9c1b32303
title: LSP: Error message when querying document symbols using python-lsp-server
